title: CLAUDE.md
text: repository (wai_ind_package) contains R package waiind (“WAI Individual Package”). package name drops underscore repo name R package names may contain letters, numbers, dots. waiind estimates Weekly Activity Index (WAI), high-frequency GDP indicator Switzerland, via Bayesian mixed-frequency dynamic factor model (hfdfm()). methodology full empirical results published Kronenberg (2026), Swiss Journal Economics Statistics, 162:10, https://doi.org/10.1186/s41937-026-00157-w, extending multi-factor framework Eckert, Kronenberg, Mikosch & Neuwirth (2025), Journal Applied Econometrics, 40(3), 270–290. Always cite two papers, earlier draft/technical-note version, describing methodology — see R/waiind-package.R README.md exact citation text. package converted pre-existing collection ad hoc research scripts proper R package across GitHub issues #9–#19 (see “Issue-tracking workflow” ); issue #20 ongoing audit R Packages (2nd ed., Wickham & Bryan, r-pkgs.org), #21 tracks CRAN-release prep (deferred — package currently GitHub-distributed ). Documentation generated roxygen2 comments; hand-edit NAMESPACE files man/ — run devtools::document() (Rscript -e 'roxygen2::roxygenise()') instead.

R CMD check ---manual <tarball> sequence doubt, since devtools::check() CI’s R CMD check diverged repo (see .Rbuildignore incident ). clean local check one commit guarantee next commit still clean — re-run , don’t assume. discipline running test suite every substantive change; treat passing R CMD check required gate git commit, optional nicety saved pre-merge. Concrete incident motivated rule: PR #27 (issue #22, claude_edits → main merge) passed every local R CMD check throughout session, real GitHub Actions CI failed 4 matrix jobs checking contents 'data' directory ... WARNING first time actually ran — warning traced file, code path, example anywhere repo (exhaustively verified: fresh clone exact failing commit SHA built clean locally zero warnings). Fixed defensively via .Rbuildignore hardening (^outputs$, ^fits$, ^data/benchmarks$, ^data/dataset$) confirmed second real CI run merging. root cause appeared never conclusively identified — exactly kind thing frequent, smaller-diff R CMD check runs (one per commit, one per branch) catch earlier make easier isolate.

text: claude-code-review.yml’s automatic per-push review disabled (see CI / workflows ) — need minimize PR pushes sake specifically. Don’t spawn subagents tasks fit direct Read/Grep/Glob calls. Batch related file changes one edit pass running R CMD check/tests, rather checking every micro-change; one check right committing still satisfies “Pre-commit verification” . Don’t re-read files already seen conversation; trust prior tool output. Don’t include @claude commit messages, PR bodies, comments unless intentionally triggering claude-code.yml. title: waiind: A High-Frequency GDP Indicator for Switzerland
text: Weekly Activity Index (WAI) high-frequency GDP indicator Switzerland: single dynamic factor, extracted Bayesian mixed-frequency dynamic factor model, directly interpretable week--week growth rate Swiss real GDP. built combine two kinds data: Conventional macroeconomic indicators — surveys, prices, trade, purchasing manager indices — published monthly quarterly, Alternative high-frequency data — payment transaction volumes, mobility indicators, search-trend indices, traffic flight counts — available daily weekly, often short histories irregular publication lags. Unlike unsupervised approaches extract factor high-frequency data alone (e.g. principal components), WAI supervised: identification restriction anchors factor observed GDP growth, direct high-frequency proxy GDP rather generic activity index merely correlates . methodology, full empirical results, real-time forecast evaluation described Kronenberg (2026); see ?waiind full citation underlying multi-factor framework Eckert, Kronenberg, Mikosch & Neuwirth (2025). vignette covers package’s user-facing workflow .

title: waiind: A High-Frequency GDP Indicator for Switzerland
text: hfdfm() estimates single latent weekly factor ftf_t whose (distributed-lag, temporally aggregated) measurement equation links every input series, whose autoregressive state equation includes stochastic volatility variance common shocks can rise crises instead smoothed away. Three features make work real-world mixed-frequency data: Data augmentation treats missing lower-frequency observations latent states, series starts 2020 updates weekly, another starts 1990 updates monthly, quarterly GDP can enter state-space model. Stochastic volatility factor state equation lets abrupt swings (e.g. COVID-19 pandemic) show genuinely higher volatility rather averaged away. Quasi-differencing measurement equation removes serial correlation series’ idiosyncratic errors, persistent indicator-specific noise doesn’t get misattributed common factor. Identification fixes factor’s loading target series (GDP) one shrinks measurement error toward zero, factor tracks GDP growth closely construction — see ?hfdfm details references, vignette("waiind")’s “Real-time GDP vintages” section target series constructed live use.

title: waiind: A High-Frequency GDP Indicator for Switzerland

what: Swiss Weekly Activity Index via a Dynamic Factor Model
title: Swiss Weekly Activity Index via a Dynamic Factor Model
text: waiind estimates weekly activity index (WAI) Switzerland produces weekly GDP nowcasts backcasts. core Bayesian mixed-frequency dynamic factor model estimated Markov chain Monte Carlo (Gibbs) sampling, combines weekly, monthly quarterly indicator series single weekly activity factor coherent quarterly real GDP. package also provides: real-time GDP vintage handling — Swiss real-time GDP vintage database ships package, --sample forecast evaluation (expanding-window backcasts, AR(1) benchmark, modified Diebold–Mariano tests), table plot generators used accompanying analysis. Note: package named waiind rather wai_ind_package R package names may contain letters, numbers dots.

title: Swiss Weekly Activity Index via a Dynamic Factor Model
text: package requires R >= 4.1.

title: Swiss Weekly Activity Index via a Dynamic Factor Model
text: fuller walkthrough model (data augmentation, stochastic volatility, GDP-identification restriction) runnable example, see vignette("waiind") — also browsable package website. curated indicator datasets ship package. small (fast, demonstration-sized) nowcast: Real-time GDP vintages work box:
code: library(waiind)  data(data_ch_dataset_test) target <- "ch.seco.gdp.real.gdp.ssa"   # quarterly real Swiss GDP  # small subset and short chain so this runs in seconds; # real runs use the full dataset and length_sample = 10000 flows  <- lapply(data_ch_dataset_test$flows[c(target, "SWISSMI")],                  stats::window, start = 2018) stocks <- lapply(data_ch_dataset_test$stocks[1:2],                  stats::window, start = 2018)  set.seed(1) fit <- hfdfm(flows = flows, stocks = stocks, target = target,              length_sample = 500, burn_in = 100)  fit$factor    # weekly activity factor (annualized growth) fit$nowcast   # quarterly GDP nowcast vintages <- get_real_time_gdp_vintages("quarterly") dat <- cut_data_real_time(data_ch_dataset_test, current_date = 2024.5,                           GDP_gr_vintages = vintages)

title: Swiss Weekly Activity Index via a Dynamic Factor Model
text: analysis/ data-raw/ directories contain scripts full research workflow (run repository root; model fits written git-ignored fits/ directory): data-raw/1_data_prep_dataset.R — builds harmonized indicator dataset raw sources (documented data-raw/README_data_prep_dataset.md); curated outputs ship package datasets data_ch_dataset / data_ch_dataset_test. analysis/2_backcast.R, analysis/real_time_backcast.R — estimate model across evaluation dates (pseudo true real-time) via run_wai_adj() / run_ar(). analysis/4_tables.R — parameter metadata tables. analysis/5_plots/ — -sample --sample evaluation: plots_analytics.R orchestrates analytics_data.R, analytics_in_sample.R analytics_out--sample.R; sample configured via wai_sample_config() (see _setup.R).

what: Estimate a high-frequency dynamic factor model — hfdfm
title: Estimate a high-frequency dynamic factor model — hfdfm
text: Estimates Bayesian mixed-frequency dynamic factor model behind Swiss Weekly Activity Index (WAI) Markov chain Monte Carlo (Gibbs) sampling. Flow stock indicator series different frequencies combined single weekly activity factor coherent low-frequency target series (typically quarterly real GDP), weekly GDP nowcasts derived.

title: Estimate a high-frequency dynamic factor model — hfdfm
code: hfdfm(   flows,   stocks,   target,   p = 1,   q = 1,   length_sample = 10000,   burn_in = 1000,   thinning = 1,   plots = FALSE,   extend_to = NULL,   stochastic_volatility = TRUE,   serial_correlation = TRUE )

title: Estimate a high-frequency dynamic factor model — hfdfm
text: flows Named list ts objects treated flow variables. Must contain target. stocks Named list ts objects treated stock variables. target Character, name low-frequency target series flows (e.g. "ch.seco.gdp.real.gdp.ssa"). p Integer, number factor lags factor state equation. q Integer, number factors. Currently ignored: sampler always uses single factor. length_sample Integer, number posterior draws keep. burn_in Integer, number initial draws discard. thinning Integer, keep every thinning-th draw burn-. plots Logical, TRUE draw base-graphics diagnostic plots data factor/volatility convergence sampling. extend_to Numeric (decimal time) NULL. beyond sample end, dataset extended zeros forecasts can produced. stochastic_volatility Logical. Currently ignored: sampler always includes stochastic volatility. Kept API compatibility. serial_correlation Logical. Currently ignored: sampler always models serial correlation measurement errors. Kept API compatibility.

title: Estimate a high-frequency dynamic factor model — hfdfm
text: object class "hfdfm": list components factor ts, posterior mean annualized activity factor. factor_var ts, posterior variance factor. index ts, posterior mean cumulated activity index. nowcast ts, posterior mean nowcast target series. nowcast_var ts, posterior variance nowcast. target Character, target series name. pars List posterior parameter means (h, lambda, phi, sigma, omega, rho, rho_var). data ts matrix prepared (standardized) data. data_augmented ts matrix augmented dataset. inventory Data frame describing series (see create_inventory()).

title: Estimate a high-frequency dynamic factor model — hfdfm
text: factor identified fixing loading target one shrinking target's measurement-error variance autocorrelation toward zero (informative priors), extracted factor closely tracks observed growth rate target rather merely correlated . resolves usual scale/sign indeterminacy dynamic factor models yields directly interpretable high-frequency proxy target series (see Kronenberg 2026, Sect. 2.4). series standardized enter uninformative priors. Missing lower-frequency observations estimated latent states via data augmentation; factor state equation includes stochastic volatility, measurement errors quasi-differenced remove serial correlation (see @references).

what: waiind: A High-Frequency GDP Indicator for Switzerland — waiind-package
title: waiind: A High-Frequency GDP Indicator for Switzerland — waiind-package
text: Estimates Weekly Activity Index (WAI): weekly GDP indicator Switzerland derived Bayesian mixed-frequency dynamic factor model (hfdfm()) combines conventional macroeconomic indicators alternative high-frequency data (mobility, transactions, search trends, similar series) weekly, monthly quarterly frequencies.

title: waiind: A High-Frequency GDP Indicator for Switzerland — waiind-package
text: model extracts single dynamic factor, constrained identification restriction fixes loading GDP one, factor directly interpretable weekly GDP growth rather generic activity index. Three features let combine heterogeneous, incomplete high-frequency data official GDP figures: Data augmentation treats missing mixed-frequency observations latent states, series can enter different starting dates, publication lags frequencies (weekly, monthly, quarterly). Stochastic volatility factor state equation lets variance common shocks vary time, crisis-period swings (e.g. COVID-19 pandemic) mechanically smoothed away. Quasi-differencing (Chib & Greenberg, 1994) removes serial correlation measurement errors, preventing persistent idiosyncratic noise individual indicators contaminating common factor. Estimation Gibbs sampling using precision sampler Chan Jeliazkov (2009), temporal aggregation flow variables following geometric-mean approximation Mariano Murasawa (2003).

title: waiind: A High-Frequency GDP Indicator for Switzerland — waiind-package
text: package ships curated indicator datasets data_ch_dataset data_ch_dataset_test, plus real-time GDP vintage database (inst/extdata/), small nowcast can estimated box - see vignette("waiind") worked walkthrough, example hfdfm() minimal version.

title: waiind: A High-Frequency GDP Indicator for Switzerland — waiind-package
text: Kronenberg, P. (2026). high-frequency GDP indicator Switzerland. Swiss Journal Economics Statistics, 162, 10. doi:10.1186/s41937-026-00157-w Eckert, F., Kronenberg, P., Mikosch, H., & Neuwirth, S. (2025). Tracking economic activity alternative high-frequency data. Journal Applied Econometrics, 40(3), 270-290. (mixed-frequency dynamic factor model underlying waiind; package estimates single-factor, GDP-identified special case used WAI.) reference list related business-cycle-indicator literature kept analysis/benchmarks/literature.md source repository.
